rulings.Case Title: GCFS, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. STEVEN W BRAGG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, October 11, 2011, Line 1, DEFENDANT STEVEN BRAGG MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND - Defendant¿s motion to vacate default and default judgment is denied. Although defendant clearly is suffering from a serious disabling condition, even given defendant¿s apparently reduced capacity, he did not act with reasonable diligence in seeking to set aside the judgment within a reasonable time. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL DIGUISEPPI VS. RUSSELL GOODROW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance With Court Order And Payment Of Missed Ime Appointment Fee And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 11, 2011, Line 1. DEFENDANT RUSSELL GOODROW'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance With Court Order And Payment Of Missed Ime Appointment Fee And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Defendant¿s motion to compel compliance with court order, payment of missed IME appointment fee, and monetary sanctions is granted as to the IME fee and denied as to the other issues. Mr. Belli failed to explain why he should not pay for the IME fee. The correct procedure to seek compliance with a court is not to seek another order confirming the first order, but to seek an order of contempt. Sanctions are denied because Mr. Belli¿s position does not lack substantial justification. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID NEUBAUER VS. UNUM GROUP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, October 11, 2011, Line 6, DEFENDANTs ROYAL MACCABEES LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY, REASSURE AMERICA LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - Defendants' motion to strike first amended complaint is denied. The fact that certain defendants have been dismissed does not, without more, render the allegations regarding those defendants subject to a motion to strike, particularly where, as here, plaintiff is pursuing a theory of conspiracy involving those defendants. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES GARDINER VS. CIRCLEPOINT, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, October 11, 2011, Line 10, DEFENDANT CIRCLEPOINT, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, SCOTT STEINWERT, JOHN O'NEIL DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT - Defendants¿ demurrer to the second amended complaint is overruled as to the first cause of action for involuntary dissolution because plaintiff has alleged that the corporation is a close corporation; sustained without leave to amend as to the second and fourth causes of action for breach of contract and conversion as to the individuals because Judge Busch previously dismissed those claims and the individuals are not liable for corporate debts; overruled as to the fourth cause of action for conversion as to the corporation because it is sufficiently pled; overruled as to fifth cause of action labeled shareholder oppression because that is a mislabeled breach of fiduciary duty cause of action that is well-pleaded; sustained without leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action because plaintiff does not allege outrageous conduct; and sustained without leave to amend as to the seventh cause of action for wrongful termination cause of action because plaintiff does not allege facts showing how his termination violated public policy. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/HEK)
